mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and new housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction sector activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series are above their 12-month moving averages, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and yields positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates above 2% annually fail to generate positive 5-day returns, or the signal reverses direction during recovery phases with a t-statistic below -2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and forces immediate negative price adjustments in the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 0.4% monthly growth threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are non-negative in 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates above 150,000 monthly fail to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength and consumer spending resilience, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of quarters where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with accelerating inflation creates stagflationary pressures that compress real corporate margins and trigger rapid equity sell-offs, resulting in negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns when both series are in their top quartiles, with forward returns averaging positive values over a 3-year sample.
